frameworks.name: feedback-as-evidence
frameworks.one_line: corrective feedback informs the learner about the actual world state

frameworks.name: feedback-as-reward (reinforcement learning)
frameworks.one_line: treats corrective feedback as reward signals teaching choice values

frameworks.name: perceptual decision-making
frameworks.one_line: framework for how humans integrate stimuli and history into choices

core_question: Do humans treat corrective feedback on perceptual decisions as evidence of world state or as reward for choices?
key_assumptions: feedback interacts with previous stimuli and choices in shaping subsequent decisions
both hypotheses can be implemented on a shared learning platform
decision strategy is adjusted trial-by-trial to improve future choices
manipulations: type of learning signal interpretation (reward vs. evidence)
presence and history of corrective feedback
joint history of past stimuli and past choices
key_findings: feedback-as-evidence model outperforms feedback-as-reward in explaining decision strategy adjustment
corrective feedback jointly shapes choices alongside past stimuli and past choices
reinforcement-learning account of perceptual feedback is insufficient for human data
interpretations: humans use corrective feedback as evidence about the world state, not as reward for their choice
perceptual feedback learning is principally distinct from reward-based decision learning
